evals.prompt: Show me the statistics for the knowledge graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json`. Use the `/graph --stats` mode. Write the complete stats JSON object (with nodes count, edges count, by_kind, and by_edge_type fields) to `stats.json`.
evals.expected_output: A stats.json file containing an object with integer counts for nodes (7) and edges (10), a by_kind array with entries for concept (3) and entity (4), and a by_edge_type array covering all 4 relationship types.

deterministic_checks.description: Stats JSON file was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
Node count is exactly 7
Edge count is exactly 10
by_kind array includes an entry for concept nodes
by_kind array includes an entry for entity nodes
Concept count in by_kind is exactly 3
Entity count in by_kind is exactly 4
by_edge_type array is present in output
by_edge_type contains exactly 4 distinct edge types

expectations: The by_edge_type array contains entries for exactly four distinct edge types and no others: contradicts (1 edge), derived-from (2 edges), mentions (6 edges), and supports (1 edge).
The skill also reports the most-connected nodes by degree, identifying urn:mif:entity:concept:update-propagation as the most-referenced entity node with 3 incoming mention edges.

evals.prompt: Rebuild the knowledge graph from the sample session findings. Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-graph.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings knowledge-graph.json` to build the graph and write the output to `knowledge-graph.json` in the current working directory.
evals.expected_output: A knowledge-graph.json file written to the outputs directory containing 7 nodes and 10 edges, all with urn:mif: identifiers, and via fields restricted to 'relationship' or 'entity'.

deterministic_checks.description: Knowledge graph JSON file was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
Graph contains exactly 7 nodes
Graph contains exactly 10 edges
Nodes array includes at least one entity-kind node
Copier concept node is present with its MIF urn
Every node id starts with urn:mif: — MIF-native invariant
Every edge via field is 'relationship' or 'entity' — no bare tag strings
@type field value 'KnowledgeGraph' is present in the output
Generator attribution references build-graph.sh

expectations: The @type field is 'KnowledgeGraph' and the generator field references 'build-graph.sh (MIF-native; SPEC §6c)', proving the output is from the correct MIF pipeline.
No edge type is a raw tag string — all edge types are MIF relationship keywords (supports, contradicts, derived-from) or 'mentions'.

evals.prompt: Look up the neighborhood of node `urn:mif:entity:concept:update-propagation` in the knowledge graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json`. Use `/graph --node urn:mif:entity:concept:update-propagation`. Write the neighborhood JSON (with node, out, and in fields) to `node.json`.
evals.expected_output: A node.json file for the update-propagation entity showing 3 incoming 'mentions' edges from all 3 concept nodes and an empty outgoing edges array.

deterministic_checks.description: Node neighborhood JSON was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
Output contains an 'in' field for incoming edges
Incoming edges include the cookiecutter concept's mention
Incoming edges include the copier concept's mention
Incoming edges include the distribution concept's mention
Exactly 3 incoming edges for the most-mentioned entity node
Outgoing edges array is empty — entity nodes are never edge sources

expectations: The .in array contains exactly 3 edges, all of type 'mentions' and via 'entity', one from each of the three concept nodes in the corpus — this entity is the most-mentioned node in the graph.
The .out array is empty because entity nodes are never edge sources in this MIF graph — outgoing edges are only emitted from concept (finding) nodes.

evals.prompt: What connects `urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-copier-0001` and `urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-distribution-0003` in the knowledge graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json`? Use `/graph --between urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-copier-0001 urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-distribution-0003`. Write the result JSON (with direct and shared fields) to `between.json`.
evals.expected_output: A between.json file showing 2 direct typed edges between the two concept nodes (one supports, one derived-from), and a shared array identifying the entity both concepts mention in common.

deterministic_checks.description: Between-nodes JSON was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
Output contains a 'direct' field for direct edges between the nodes
Direct edges include the supports relationship from copier-0001 to distribution-0003
Direct edges include the derived-from relationship from distribution-0003 back to copier-0001
Exactly 2 direct edges connect the two nodes
Shared neighbors include urn:mif:entity:concept:update-propagation, the entity both concepts mention

expectations: The supports edge in .direct has source copier-0001 and target distribution-0003; the derived-from edge has the reverse direction — distribution-0003 back to copier-0001 — confirming a bidirectional relationship.
The .shared field is a non-empty array of entity URNs, identifying the common conceptual ground both findings share.

evals.prompt: List all entity nodes in the knowledge graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json` with their degree. Use `/graph --kind entity`. Write the entity list as a JSON array (not a wrapped object) to `entities.json`.
evals.expected_output: An entities.json file containing a JSON array of 4 entity objects, each with an id starting with urn:mif:entity: and a degree or edge-count field.

deterministic_checks.description: Entity list JSON was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
Array contains exactly 4 entity entries
Array includes the copier technology entity by its exact MIF urn
All entity IDs begin with urn:mif:entity:

expectations: All 4 entity IDs in the array begin with 'urn:mif:entity:' — no raw label strings or non-MIF identifiers appear.
Each entry includes a degree field or equivalent showing the count of edges connecting to it; update-propagation has the highest degree (3).
The four entities are: urn:mif:entity:concept:living-template, urn:mif:entity:concept:update-propagation, urn:mif:entity:technology:cookiecutter, and urn:mif:entity:technology:copier.

evals.prompt: Render an HTML visualization of the knowledge graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json`. Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-graph-viz.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json knowledge-graph.html` to produce the HTML and write it to `knowledge-graph.html` in the current working directory.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained knowledge-graph.html file that embeds node and edge data inline, contains labels for all 7 nodes, and shows the graph as 7 nodes and 10 edges.

deterministic_checks.description: HTML visualization was written to the outputs directory
HTML title or heading contains 'Knowledge graph'
HTML contains the Copier node label
HTML embeds the raw urn:mif: identifiers, proving the graph data is inlined
HTML does not load from external CDN — self-contained requirement

expectations: The HTML is fully self-contained — the graph JSON data is embedded inline in a <script type='application/json'> block or equivalent and no external CDN or network fetch is required.
The heading or title shows '7 nodes, 10 edges', matching the actual sample graph counts from the embedded data.

evals.prompt: Look up the neighborhood of concept node `urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-copier-0001` in the knowledge graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json`. Use `/graph --node urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-copier-0001`. Write the neighborhood JSON (with node, out, and in fields) to `node.json`.
evals.expected_output: A node.json file for the copier concept node showing outgoing edges including a supports edge to distribution-0003, and incoming edges including a contradicts from cookiecutter-0002 and a derived-from from distribution-0003.

deterministic_checks.description: Node neighborhood JSON was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
Outgoing edges include the supports relationship to distribution-0003
Incoming edges include the contradicts relationship from cookiecutter-0002
Incoming edges include the derived-from relationship from distribution-0003
Exactly 2 incoming edges for the copier concept node

expectations: The supports edge in .out has target 'urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-distribution-0003', confirming the copier → distribution directional relationship.
The .in array has exactly 2 edges: a contradicts from urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-cookiecutter-0002 and a derived-from from urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-distribution-0003.

evals.prompt: Build the cross-topic concordance from the sample session data. Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-concordance.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta concordance.json` to produce `concordance.json` in the current working directory. The sample session findings live at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/`.
evals.expected_output: A concordance.json file with @type 'Concordance', 7 nodes (3 concept + 4 entity) and 10 edges, where concept nodes carry verdict, topics, entityType, ontology, and flagged fields — distinguishing the concordance from a plain knowledge-graph.json.

deterministic_checks.description: Concordance JSON file was written to the outputs directory
Output is valid JSON
@type is 'Concordance' — not 'KnowledgeGraph'
Generator attribution references build-concordance.sh
Concordance contains exactly 7 nodes
Concordance contains exactly 10 edges
Concept nodes carry a 'topics' array — concordance-specific field absent in knowledge-graph.json
Concept nodes carry a 'verdict' field
All 3 survived concepts are topic 'sample-session'
All concept verdicts are 'survived' (3 findings, all survived)

expectations: The concordance differentiates from a plain knowledge graph by carrying per-concept verdict (survived), flagged (false), topics (["sample-session"]), entityType (null — no ontology bound), and ontology (null) fields on every concept node.
The generator field in the concordance JSON explicitly references 'build-concordance.sh (MIF-native ontological spine; SPEC §8d)', confirming the correct script was used.

evals.prompt: Validate the concordance built from the sample session. First build it: `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-concordance.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta concordance.json`. Then validate: `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/validate-concordance.sh concordance.json > validate.txt 2>&1`. Write the validation output to `validate.txt`.
evals.expected_output: validate.txt containing 'conformant' — the sample findings are all untyped (entityType null), so no undeclared domain type violations exist. Exit code 0.

deterministic_checks.description: Concordance was built before validation
Validation output file was written
Validation reports conformant — exit 0 for an untyped-but-valid corpus
No conformance violations on the all-untyped sample corpus
Validator did not abort due to missing catalog or config

expectations: The validate-concordance script exits 0 and prints exactly one line of the form 'validate-concordance: conformant (7 nodes, 10 edges)', with the node and edge counts matching the built concordance.

The validation output does NOT contain '/ontology-review' — the untyped sample findings are valid (entityType null passes the node loop filter), so the script identifies no gaps requiring ontology binding.

evals.prompt: Show corpus-wide stats for the concordance. Build it first: `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-concordance.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta concordance.json`. Then compute concordance stats with `/graph --concordance --stats` using: `jq '{nodes:(.nodes|length),edges:(.edges|length),by_kind:[.nodes|group_by(.kind)[]|{kind:.[0].kind,count:length}],by_verdict:[.nodes|map(select(.kind=="concept"))|group_by(.verdict)[]|{verdict:(.[0].verdict//"untyped"),count:length}],by_topic:[.nodes|map(.topics[])|group_by(.)[]|{topic:.[0],count:length}]}' concordance.json > stats.json`.
evals.expected_output: A stats.json with nodes=7, edges=10, by_kind showing concept:3 and entity:4, by_verdict showing survived:3, and by_topic showing sample-session:7.

deterministic_checks.description: Concordance stats JSON was written
Output is valid JSON
Total node count is 7
Total edge count is 10
by_kind shows 3 concept nodes
by_kind shows 4 entity nodes
by_verdict shows all 3 concepts survived
by_topic shows 7 nodes attributed to sample-session

expectations: The by_verdict breakdown is unique to the concordance — it would not exist in plain per-topic graph stats — confirming the skill operated on concordance.json, not knowledge-graph.json.
The by_topic field confirms the concordance spans topics correctly; with a single-topic corpus, 'sample-session' accounts for all 7 nodes.

evals.prompt: After building the concordance (`bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-concordance.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta concordance.json`), use `/graph --concordance` to detect coverage gaps — topics with no bound ontology (untyped concept nodes). Run: `jq -r '[.nodes[] | select(.kind=="concept" and .entityType==null) | .topics[]] | unique[]' concordance.json > gaps.txt`. Then report any topics that need `/ontology-review --topic <id> --enrich` to bind an ontology.
evals.expected_output: A gaps.txt file containing 'sample-session', identifying it as a topic with untyped concept nodes (entityType null). The skill recommends running `/ontology-review --topic sample-session --enrich`.

deterministic_checks.description: Coverage gap report file was written
sample-session is identified as a topic with untyped concept nodes
Concordance was built before gap detection
Exactly 1 untyped topic in the sample corpus
deterministic_checks.command: jq -e '[.nodes[] | select(.kind=="concept" and .entityType==null) | .topics[]] | unique | length == 1' concordance.json

expectations: The skill surfaces 'sample-session' as a topic with untyped findings and recommends running `/ontology-review --topic sample-session --enrich` to bind/enrich the ontology, then rebuild with `--concordance --build`.
The skill distinguishes this as a soft gap (missing ontology binding), not a hard conformance violation — it notes that untyped findings are valid nodes but leaves the spine under-typed.
